description: TypeScript medical records validation - HIPAA regulatory standards

codingStandards.description: Quality gates for HIPAA-compliant TypeScript code. Run BEFORE every commit.
preCommitChecklist: 1. npm run build OR npx tsc -- must compile with zero errors
2. npm test OR npx jest -- all tests must pass
3. npx tsc --noEmit --strict -- enable strict mode checks

Type Safety: NEVER use 'any' type -- use proper interfaces or unknown
Define interfaces for all data structures (e.g. MedicalRecord, ValidationResult)
Enable strict mode in tsconfig.json (strict: true)
Code Architecture: Separate concerns into modules: src/types.ts, src/parser.ts, src/validator.ts, src/cli.ts
src/cli.ts is the entrypoint -- it calls functions from other modules
Keep every function under 30 lines -- extract helpers for complex logic
Export validation functions so tests can call them directly without spawning a process
Use pure functions: input -> output, no side effects in validation logic
Error Handling: Define a ValidationError interface with line number, message, and category
Collect all errors in a single pass -- do not exit on first failure
Return meaningful exit codes: 0=success, 1=validation failure, 2=runtime error
Wrap file I/O in try-catch; propagate structured errors to CLI layer
Documentation: Every exported function needs a JSDoc comment with @param and @returns
Every interface needs a JSDoc comment explaining its purpose

testStandards.description: Minimum test quality for regulatory compliance.
structure: Import and test validation functions directly -- not only via CLI spawn
Use test fixtures in tests/fixtures/ for data-driven tests
minimumCases: Happy path: valid input returns success
Empty file: zero records is valid
Malformed JSON: parser returns line-level errors
Invalid recordType: validator rejects unknown types
File not found: CLI exits with code 2
Mixed valid and invalid records: all errors reported
rules: Minimum 3 test cases per requirement (happy, error, edge)
Assert specific values (line numbers, error messages) -- not just truthy/falsy
Test the function, not just the CLI exit code -- unit tests over integration tests

preSubmissionChecklist.description: QA will REJECT your submission if ANY of these are true. Self-check before sending completion report.
checks: npx tsc --noEmit --strict fails (code must compile in strict mode)
Any test fails (npm test must exit 0)
'any' type used anywhere in src/ (grep -rn ': any' src/ must return nothing)
All logic in a single file -- src/ must have multiple .ts files (types, parser, validator, cli)
Fewer than 3 test cases per requirement
Tests only spawn CLI process -- must also have unit tests that import validation functions directly
No TypeScript interfaces defined for data structures
Missing REQ-HCDP-XXX traceability comments in tests/
Acceptance criteria from the task not fully implemented

projectContext: HIPAA Security Rule (45 CFR 164) compliance required
Medical records validation CLI for healthcare data pipeline
JSONL format: one JSON object per line
REQ-HCDP-XXX traceability mandatory in all test files
